input.prompt:
I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make
this page theme-aware in XAML and explain the C# alternative.

input.initialPrompt:
I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make
this page theme-aware in XAML and explain the C# alternative.

data.content:
## AppThemeBinding in .NET MAUI

**XAML — use `AppThemeBinding` markup extension:**

```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
             x:Class="MyApp.MainPage"
             BackgroundColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#1C1C1E}">

    <VerticalStackLayout Padding="20" Spacing="16">

        <Label Text="Welcome"
               FontSize="28"
               TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#000000, Dark=#FFFFFF}" />

        <Label Text="This page adapts to your system theme."
               FontSize="16"
               TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#444444, Dark=#BBBBBB}" />

        <Button Text="Get Started"
                BackgroundColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#6200EE, Dark=#BB86FC}"
                TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#000000}" />

    </VerticalStackLayout>
</ContentPage>
```

The framework reads the device's current theme and picks `Light` or `Dark` automatically — **no event handling needed**. When the user toggles the system theme, values update in real time.

---

**C# equivalent — use `SetAppTheme` extension method:**

```csharp
public class MainPage : ContentPage
{
    public MainPage()
    {
        // Page background
        this.SetAppTheme(BackgroundColorProperty,
            Color.FromArgb("#FFFFFF"),   // light
            Color.FromArgb("#1C1C1E")); // dark

        var title = new Label { Text = "Welcome", FontSize = 28 };
        title.SetAppTheme(Label.TextColorProperty,
            Color.FromArgb("#000000"),
            Color.FromArgb("#FFFFFF"));

        var subtitle = new Label { Text = "This page adapts to your system theme.", FontSize = 16 };
        subtitle.SetAppTheme(Label.TextColorProperty,
            Color.FromArgb("#444444"),
            Color.FromArgb("#BBBBBB"));

        var button = new Button { Text = "Get Started" };
        button.SetAppTheme(Button.BackgroundColorProperty,
            Color.FromArgb("#6200EE"),
            Color.FromArgb("#BB86FC"));
        button.SetAppTheme(Button.TextColorProperty,
            Color.FromArgb("#FFFFFF"),
            Color.FromArgb("#000000"));

        Content = new VerticalStackLayout
        {
            Padding = 20, Spacing = 16,
            Children = { title, subtitle, button }
        };
    }
}
```

---

**Key points:**

| | XAML | C# |
|---|---|---|
| Syntax | `{AppThemeBinding Light=…, Dark=…}` | `view.SetAppTheme(property, light, dark)` |
| Works on | Any bindable property (colors, images, strings, etc.) | Same |
| Reacts live | ✅ | ✅ |

**Scaling tip:** For many themed values, define them once in `App.xaml` resources and reference by key:

```xml
<!-- App.xaml -->
<Color x:Key="PageBg">{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#1C1C1E}</Color>

<!-- MainPage.xaml -->
<ContentPage BackgroundColor="{StaticResource PageBg}" />
```

This keeps colors in one place instead of scattered across pages.
